rag_rules: Fix unresolved references or invalid expressions in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are visible and properly typed.
Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in the same annex for AGREE expressions.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, ensure type consistency between left and right sides of expressions.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers not present in the model.
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes after subcomponents and connections.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope; use assign for output features or auxiliary variables.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Constants and equations in AGREE must use valid scalar types: int, real, bool.
Left-hand side of assignment in implementation scope must be an output feature or declared auxiliary variable.
anti_patterns: Do not reference identifiers or types that are not visible in the current AADL scope.
Do not mix incompatible types in binary expressions such as bool = <error>.
